title: POV Taxonomy File
description: Schema for a single point-of-view (POV) taxonomy file in the AI Triad Research project. Each POV camp (accelerationist, safetyist, skeptic) has one file conforming to this schema.

_schema_version.description: Semantic version of the schema this file conforms to.

_doc.description: Human-readable documentation string describing this file's purpose and editing conventions.

pov.description: The point-of-view camp this taxonomy represents.

color_hex.description: Hex color code used to visually distinguish this POV in the editor UI.

last_modified.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the last modification to this file.

nodes.description: Ordered list of taxonomy nodes belonging to this POV.

PovNode.description: A single taxonomy node representing a claim, value, data point, or methodological position within a POV camp.

id.description: Unique identifier for this node. Format: {pov_prefix}-{category_prefix}-{sequence}. POV prefixes: acc (accelerationist), saf (safetyist), skp (skeptic). Category prefixes: goals (Goals/Values), data (Data/Facts), methods (Methods/Arguments).

category.description: The epistemic category this node belongs to. Goals/Values: normative positions about what should happen. Data/Facts: empirical claims about what is or will be. Methods/Arguments: what someone is arguing for or against, and the methods, logic models, interpretive lenses, or policy approaches they use to make those arguments.

label.description: Short human-readable title for this node. Should be concise enough to display in a list view.

description.description: Full description of the claim, value, data point, or methodological position. Should be a self-contained paragraph that can be understood without reading other nodes.

parent_id.description: ID of the parent node if this node is a sub-claim. Null for top-level nodes. Must reference another node ID within the same POV file.

parent_relationship.description: The ontological relationship between this node and its parent. is_a: this node is a more specific version of the parent. part_of: this node is a component or aspect of the parent. specializes: this node is a concrete implementation of the parent's general principle. Null when parent_id is null.

parent_rationale.description: 1-2 sentence explanation of why this node is placed under its parent with the specified relationship type. Null when parent_id is null.

children.description: Ordered list of child node IDs. Must reference node IDs within the same POV file. Inverse of parent_id.

cross_cutting_refs.description: List of cross-cutting node IDs that this node relates to. References nodes in the cross-cutting.json file.

conflict_ids.description: Optional list of conflict claim IDs that this node is involved in. References conflict files in the conflicts/ directory.

graph_attributes.description: Rich analytical attributes generated by Invoke-AttributeExtraction. Optional — populated organically.

node_scope.description: AIF-aligned classification of the node's role in argumentation. claim: a specific assertion. scheme: an argumentative strategy. bridging: connects claims to schemes.

epistemic_type.description: The epistemic category of the node's content (e.g., empirical_claim, normative_prescription).

steelman_vulnerability.description: The strongest counterargument against the strongest version of this claim.
